etymology.description: Phonetic: 是(是) (shì) + Semantic: 頁(頁) (yè, "head") = represents 'forehead' -> elaborated to mean 'topic; heading'
etymology.interpretation: The character 題(題) integrates 是(是) (shì, "is") as the phonetic component and 頁(頁) (yè, "head") as the semantic component, hinting at the idea of referring to something conceptual on a 'figurative forehead'. Over time it evolved to mean 'topic' or 'title'.
etymology.simplification: The character 題(題) was simplified to 题 by replacing 頁(頁) with its simplified form 页(页), reducing the number of strokes while maintaining the phonetic component 是(是).

examples.chinese: 老师给我们布置了一道有趣的题(老師給我們佈置了一道有趣的題)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎoshī gěi wǒmen bùzhìle yī dào yǒuqù de tí.
examples.english: The teacher assigned us an interesting topic.

examples.chinese: 面对复杂的题(面對複雜的題)，他显得有些不知所措(他顯得有些不知所措)。
examples.pinyin: Miànduì fùzá de tí, tā xiǎndé yǒuxiē bù zhī suǒ cuò.
examples.english: Faced with the complex subject, he seemed a bit overwhelmed.

examples.chinese: 这篇文章的题很吸引人(這篇文章的題很吸引人)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè piān wénzhāng de tí hěn xīyǐn rén.
examples.english: The title of this article is very captivating.
